ranked_movies.title: Mad Max: Fury Road
ranked_movies.reason: Extensively discussed across multiple threads for its action sequences, thematic depth, and as a prime example of the 'insane movie' genre with non-stop action. Also highlighted in discussions about emotional trauma and character development.

ranked_movies.title: The Shining
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently referenced in discussions about horror and film technique. Praised for its atmosphere, cinematography, and as a masterclass in horror filmmaking. Multiple comments analyze its unique qualities.

ranked_movies.title: Mad Max: The Road Warrior
ranked_movies.reason: Central to the discussion on action films and emotional trauma. Recognized as a classic in the genre and directly compared to 'Fury Road' in comments.

ranked_movies.title: Everything Everywhere All at Once
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as an example of a movie where 'crazy stuff doesn't stop.' Also noted for its creative action and emotional depth.

ranked_movies.title: Pulp Fiction
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a classic example of a film with non-stop, crazy events. One of the top comments in the 'insane movie' thread.

ranked_movies.title: The Exorcist
ranked_movies.reason: Brought up in the context of horror and compared to 'The Shining' for its impact. Also mentioned in discussions about film technique.

ranked_movies.title: Aliens
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned in the context of character likability and as a classic action-horror hybrid. Also connected to discussions of 'The Road Warrior'.

ranked_movies.title: Requiem for a Dream
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as having one of the most gloomy conclusions, a point of consensus among multiple comments.
